results.title: The Workflow Bottleneck

finalPrediction.nextLikelyBehavior: Likely to engage constructively and define a more concrete next step if the rep stays relevant.
predictedDrivers: Next-step intent was signaled but not clearly asked, so the HCP may offer passive agreement without action.
This HCP pattern quickly converts frustration into disengagement.
finalPrediction.predictedEngagementPattern: HCP is actively participating and building on rep input.

contextProfile.practiceSetting: Dermatologist — Solo or small group practice, limited staff, no prior experience with the administration or monitoring requirements
contextProfile.patientMixReality: The relevant patients are the ones whose care path creates extra operational work for staff.
contextProfile.accessFrictionType: Access is not the dominant blocker unless the rep makes it one.
contextProfile.staffingReality: Staff capacity is limited, so any extra monitoring, callbacks, or paperwork will be judged immediately.
contextProfile.workflowBottleneck: The HCP is deciding whether this adds a real staff step, handoff, or monitoring burden.
contextProfile.adoptionStyle: Will engage if the rep stays specific and useful.
contextProfile.evidenceSensitivity: Sensitive to whether the evidence matters in practice, not just on paper.

capabilityDrivers.influence: Rep responded directly to HCP concerns — resistance is decreasing, openness is increasing.
Open, relevant questions are driving more specific and engaged HCP responses.
Rep connected to HCP's real-world context — HCP is engaging with practice-relevant responses.
Objections were acknowledged and explored — they are softening and becoming more specific.
Rep adjusted approach as the conversation evolved — HCP behavior is shifting positively.
Rep hinted at next steps without securing a specific commitment — passive agreement is likely.

results.briefRationale: Deterministic QA review: no missed capability signals detected. Effective signals observed in Question Quality, Listening & Responsiveness, Value Framing, Objection Handling, Conversation Control & Structure, Adaptability.
